pages.full_text: It has been a very wet, very warm weekend. The campsite * * * * * is owned by Colonel Carter, who lives in a lovely Cotswold house in the park. On Saturday we walked into the village + saw a very posh restaurant Gibbons, well worth a visit, + then went on to the Cross pub, for lunch, steak + kidney pies, delicious chips + curries. We walked, picking blackberries, up old lanes, lots of 2 mile walks in the area, + met a very chatty farmer, who fortunately turned out to be friendly. The footpath had apparently been changed. He told us that we had walked past Princess Anne's house. In fact we [continues on right page] a fine view of it from our
pages.notes: Five asterisks (* * * * *) appear above/near the word 'campsite' — possibly a rating or decorative mark. The text at the bottom is cut off and continues on the right page. 'Gibbons' could be a restaurant name or proprietor name. The village is not explicitly named but is near the campsite in what appears to be the Cotswolds/Avening area based on right page context.

pages.full_text:
[continued from left] bedroom.

We had tea when we got back, + the Col. scared me half to death bringing me [that] a guide to the church when he suddenly appeared at the window. His wife is [cut off at bottom]

[Sketch caption:] Gatscombe Park – view from the camping van in Avening Park.

[Sketch caption:] Window of Avening Park house
photo_captions: Gatscombe Park – view from the camping van in Avening Park.
pages.notes: The right page contains two hand-drawn pen sketches: (1) a drawing of a large house/estate building (Gatscombe Park) with trees and landscape, captioned 'Gatscombe Park – view from the camping van in Avening Park.'; (2) a sketch of a decorative window with caption 'Window of Avening Park house'. These are artistic sketches, not route maps. The word 'that' appears struck through in the text ('bringing me ~~that~~ a guide'). The text at the very bottom is cut off. The left page text ends mid-sentence continuing with 'bedroom.' at the top of this page — referring to a view of Princess Anne's house (Gatscombe Park) from the camping van bedroom window. Gatscombe Park is Princess Anne's (Princess Royal's) estate near Minchinhampton/Avening in Gloucestershire.
